articles.title: Heavy rains continue to lash Kerala; IMD issues orange alert in 7 districts - Mid-day
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Tripura
articles.full_text:
Heavy rains continue to lash Kerala; IMD issues orange alert in 7 districts
Heavy rainfall continued across Kerala, causing waterlogging and flooding in low-lying areas. The IMD has issued an orange alert for seven districts and warned of thunderstorms, strong winds and very heavy rain in several parts of the state
Representational Image
Heavy rains continued to lash several parts of Kerala, waterlogging roads and flooding low-lying areas, with the IMD on Friday issuing an orange alert in seven districts of the state.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued an orange alert in Pathanamthitta, Alappuzha, Kottayam, Idukki, Ernakulam, Thrissur and Kasaragod districts of the state.

articles.title: Tripura Govt decides to close all schools due to heatwave conditions - News On AIR
articles.full_text:
In Tripura, prevailing heatwave conditions, the state government has decided to close all the schools for next four days.
According to a press statement issued by Education (School) Secretary, N.C. Sharma, all government, government aided, schools under TTAADC and privately managed schools will remain closed from April 24-27 in the state.
In another, press communique, the state revenue department has said that the medical fraternity has asked people to avoid exposure to the blazing sun and heat which might in worst case lead to sun strokes.
The district administrations have been advised to activate the emergency operation centres, keep alerting the quick response teams and resources, ensuring uninterrupted power supply to all areas and taking support from other key agencies.
